rows.notes: Year header clearly visible at top of page in large numerals.

rows.description: Computus Rectoris a 2do die Novembris A.D. 1859 ad cundem diem revolutum A.D. 1860
rows.notes: Latin header establishing the accounting period. Handwriting is legible Latin ecclesiastical text. No monetary amounts.
Section header 'Recepta' (Receipts) in clear handwriting. Structural label introducing the receipts section below.

rows.description: Clutterbucke 8 & ½ [payment] on Corn Underwood 4 & 9 on 8d
rows.notes: First receipt entry. Pounds column shows '10', shillings shows '30' (exceeds valid range of 0–19). This suggests column misalignment. Re-examining: the layout may have the shillings bleeding into adjacent space. The rightmost visible amount appears to be '8 6' which could indicate 8 shillings 6 pence for part of the entry. High uncertainty due to complex entry structure and apparent multiple amounts on one line. Interpreting as primary amounts on right margin: 10 pounds. Secondary amounts uncertain.
Short entry, likely sub-item. Shows amount in right margin as '8 6'. Clear alignment with shillings and pence columns. No pounds visible for this specific line.
Amount visible on right: '13 8 7'. Pounds clearly '13', shillings '8' (valid), pence '7' (valid). Handwriting legible but entry description is abbreviated/unclear.

Partially legible entry text. Amount shows '19 8 6' with what appears to be a fraction mark. Shillings and pence are valid; pounds appear to be 19 (high but plausible). Pence fraction may indicate half-penny but symbol is faint.
Shillings shows '13' which is valid (0–19 range). Entry name is unclear handwriting. Amount '17 13 4' appears to be read from right margin. All values in valid currency ranges.

rows.description: Die in 8d Jones consid in paid 5S
rows.notes: Legible entry text but handwriting is connected/flowing. Amount '17 2 9' clearly within valid ranges. Pence '9' is valid (0–11).
Legible institutional entry. Amount '15 13 7' is clear in right margin. All currency values valid.
Building fund entry. Amount '4 7 1' visible in margin. One digit uncertain in entry description but amount is clear and valid.
Amount shows '10' only visible in pounds column. Shillings and pence columns appear blank for this entry. Could indicate a round pound figure or sub-item allocation.
Rent receipt entry. Amount '11 8 6' clearly aligned. All values in valid ranges. Handwriting legible.
Entry text partially unclear (abbreviated/cursive). Amount '7 11 8' is clear in columns and valid. Pence '8' is within range 0–11.
Legible entry. Amount '3 3 2' is clear and all values valid. Simple three-digit reading with no ambiguity.

rows.description: Disposer Room Rent & Klines 1858 & Do 1859. hate
rows.notes: Multi-year room rent entry. Amount shows '7 11' visible; pence column appears blank or unaligned. Partial reading due to layout complexity.

rows.description: Repayment of Monies Borrowed by hater from Churchek Fund
rows.notes: Loan repayment entry. Amount '39 4 5' clearly visible in right margin. All currency values in valid ranges. Entry text is legible institutional description.
Interest entry. Amount shows '4 2' with what appears to be 'ob' (half-penny) fraction. Handwriting for description is faint/unclear. Confidence lowered due to illegible text.
Choir fund repayment. Amount shows '9' in pounds column only. Shillings and pence appear blank. Could indicate round pound figure.
Sale proceeds entry for lantern fund. Amount '19 10 8' visible in right margin. All values in valid ranges. '2000' may refer to quantity or account code, not the amount.
Minor entry for keys repair/amendment. Amount shows '1' in shillings only. Pounds and pence columns appear blank. Small maintenance cost.
Personal loan repayment. Amount shows '5' in pounds only. Entry name partially unclear (Allnnett or similar) but amount is clear.
Rector repayment entry. Amount shows '30' in what may be pounds or shillings column. Entry text appears cut off or incomplete (ends with 'hoi'). Interpretation of column assignment uncertain.

Final total line at bottom of receipts section. Amount '6726 11 7' is clearly visible and well-aligned in columns. This is the sum of all receipts above. High confidence due to clear spacing and alignment of total figures.
